_purpose: This is the controlled vocabulary shared between the AI Coach practice case library (cases-*.json) and the future AI Case Management module. Every practice case is tagged with an objectionType; when Case Management ships, real client cases logged by agents should use this SAME vocabulary so the two systems can cross-reference each other (e.g. 'this real case matches practice scenario insurance-06 — practice it before your next call').

low_resilience.zh: 抗壓性低、易被拒絕打擊

2.en: Intermediate — some resistance, needs skill to redirect

3.en: Advanced — high resistance, emotionally charged or high-stakes

howToUseWhenBuildingCaseManagement: When an agent logs a real client case, let them (or an AI classifier) pick the closest objectionType from this same list, instead of free text.

Store the picked practice case id + tags in sessionStorage/localStorage when a practice session starts (see simulator.html: obPickedClient._id / obPickedClient._tags) so future features can read 'what did this user practice recently' without re-architecting the data model.
A simple v1 binding: if a real case's objectionType matches a practice case the agent hasn't done recently, surface it as a suggested practice before their next call with that client.
A simple v2 binding: after a practice session, if performance was weak, suggest the agent flag any real client in Case Management with the same objectionType for extra follow-up attention.
